questions.statement: Which of these statements about motivation and learning is correct?
options: Learning is effective when the students are motivated to avoid punishment&nbsp;&nbsp; /
Learning is effective when the students have intrinsic motivation /
Learning is effective when the students are extrinsically motivation&nbsp; /
Motivation does not have any role to play in learning /

questions.statement: <span style="">Teacher beliefs about success/failures of students, often influence the learning and motivation of&nbsp;</span><span style="">students, especially from the disadvantaged group. Which of the following statements is correct in&nbsp;</span><span style="">this context?</span>
options: Teacher expectancy influences children&#39;s learning significantly. /
Teachers need to control student autonomy within the classroom. /
Teacher should encourage children to &lsquo;perform&rsquo; better than others and complete well. /
Teachers need to train such students in strategies to &lsquo;avoid&#39; failure at any cost. /

questions.statement: The method of teaching is chosen on the basis of________
Is one of the factors that&nbsp; how children learn and grow Assimilation means:
options: Process of taking in new information into our already existing schemas&nbsp; /
Changing altering our existing schemas in light of new information&nbsp; /
Moving from one stage of thought to the next /
Modifying existing schemas, or ideas, as a result of new information or new experiences.&nbsp; /
questions.explanation: <strong>&#39;Jean Piaget&#39;</strong>, a swiss psychologist, belongs to the cognitive school of psychology, is famous for his<strong> work on child development</strong>. In his &#39;<strong>theory of cognitive development,</strong>&#39; Piaget proposed the concept of<strong> &#39;assimilation&#39;</strong> that refers to the <strong>process of taking in new information into our already existing schemas. </strong>It manages the way an individual <strong>takes and interprets new information</strong> in pre-existing mental structures without modifying them.&nbsp;

questions.statement: Which aspect of counseling and guidance is particularly important for slow learners?&nbsp;
options: Accelerating their learning to catch up with their peers.&nbsp; /
<span style="color: rgb(33, 37, 41);">Focusing solely on academic improvement.&nbsp;</span> /
<span style="color: rgb(33, 37, 41);">Providing emotional support and building self-confidence.</span> /
<span style="color: rgb(33, 37, 41);">&nbsp;Assigning more homework to compensate for their slow progress.</span> /
